text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little village, there was a peculiar old woman named Miss Hattie. She had a garden filled with flowers, but no pet animals. Instead, she had a collection of unusual statues—medieval knights, fairies, and a wise old owl.

One peculiar day, Miss Hattie decided to organize a tournament in her garden. The only rule? It had to be a contest of wit and cleverness. She called out for participants: "Come all ye bright-hearted intellectuals! I'll pay a crown for the cleverest talk!"

Various villagers came forward, each showing off their peculiar knowledge—how to weave spells, the Latin names of mythical beasts, the local gossip. Then, after much debate and playful bickering, it was decided: the best wit wins.

The first round was a whirlwind. The knight joked about being feared by all foes single-handedly because "all opposition flees!" The fairy giggled about moonlit treasures.
